subject: Math

lesson.story_mission: Repair the Sky Number Line from 0 to 120.
lesson.mini_lesson: When we cross a decade, the tens digit changes and counting keeps a steady +1 or -1 step.

worked_examples.text: 99, 100, 101: after 99, the tens and ones reset to make 100.
110, 109, 108: counting backward decreases by one each step.

hint_ladder: Say the numbers aloud one by one.
Check whether you are adding 1 or subtracting 1.
Watch the tens digit at decade transitions like 99→100 and 109→110.

guided_practice.prompt: Build the next three numbers after 97.

question_audio.text: Build the next three numbers after 97.
question_audio.label: Read Question

adaptive_question_bank.prompt: Which sequence counts by ones correctly?

question_audio.text: Which sequence counts by ones correctly?
question_audio.label: Read Question

adaptive_question_bank.prompt: Count backward from 32 for three numbers.

question_audio.text: Count backward from 32 for three numbers.
question_audio.label: Read Question

misconception_bank.decade_transition_skip: Learner loses track when tens digit changes at decade boundaries.
misconception_bank.digit_reversal: Learner reverses or misreads digit order in two-digit numbers.
misconception_bank.backward_count_drop: Learner skips numbers or changes direction when counting backward.

review_bank.prompt: Practice crossing 99→100 and 109→110 on a number line.

story.text: Welcome to Skyline Counter Quest. The Sky Number Line needs repairs from 0 to 120. Your goal is to count forward and backward by one, even when the numbers cross a new ten. You will learn to listen for the counting pattern and watch what changes in the tens and ones places. Press Start Mission when you are ready.
story.label: Read This Page

lesson.text: This lesson is about counting forward and backward within 120. Important words are forward, backward, number line, tens, ones, and decade. The number line model shows each step moving by plus one or minus one. A common mistake is jumping over a decade number, like forgetting that 99 goes to 100. Press Next to continue.
lesson.label: Read This Page

watch.text: Watch this worked example. The prompt shows 99, 100, 101, so the model is counting forward one number at a time. Step one, read the numbers in order. Step two, notice that after 99, the tens and ones reset to make 100. Step three, check the pattern: each step is plus one. The final answer after 99 is 100, and after 100 is 101. Press Next to continue.
watch.label: Read This Page

demo.text: This demo shows how to count on a number line. Watch the jumps move one space at a time, not two or three spaces. For example, 18, 19, 20, 21 counts forward by plus one each time. Press Next to continue.
demo.label: Read This Page

practice.text: This is practice. Use the count-by-one strategy and say the numbers aloud in order. The model shows a number line, so each move forward adds one and each move backward subtracts one. You can press Read Question to hear the exact question again. Choose your answer when you are ready.
practice.label: Read This Page

challenge.text: This is a challenge. It is harder because the count may cross a decade, like 99 to 100, or move backward from a larger number. Use the number line and check whether you are adding one or subtracting one. You can press Read Question to hear the exact question, and use the hint if you need help.
challenge.label: Read This Page

checkpoint.text: This is your checkpoint. Show that you can find the next number when counting forward or backward within 120. Use the number line pattern, say the numbers aloud, and watch the tens digit at decade changes. Press Read Question if you want the exact question read aloud. Answer carefully, then continue.
checkpoint.label: Read This Page

badge.text: Great work repairing the Sky Number Line. You practiced counting forward, counting backward, and crossing decade numbers like 99 to 100. Those skills help you read and write longer number patterns. Press Next to see your profile.
badge.label: Read This Page

profile.text: This is your growth profile. It can summarize your attempts, correct answers, and accuracy so you know how your counting practice went. Your next recommendation will help you keep strengthening number-line counting. You can replay the mission or go back to the hub.
profile.label: Read This Page
